commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh How can you say, I am not polluted, I have not gone after Baalim. see your way in the valley, know what you have done: you are a swift dromedary traversing her ways; Valley of Hinnom, under the very walls of Jerusalem. (Calmet) Runner. Hebrew, "dromedary "which takes its name from its swiftness. (Haydock) The female camel continues all day with the male, and cannot be approached. (Aristotle; Pliny, x. ) Juda is represented as no less libidinous.
